expectations.text: Identifies that hold_soc_state and its expiry timer are in-memory only and lost on restart/reload
expectations.evidence: "tracks the hold only in in-memory state (hold_soc_state dict + an asyncio expiry timer)"; "timer and state die with the process"

expectations.text: Identifies that persist_force_mode_state serializes only force charge/discharge (Hold writes None)
expectations.evidence: "persist_force_mode_state() ... serializes only force charge/discharge — with only Hold active it writes force_mode_state = None, affirmatively recording 'nothing active' (and clobbering any prior persisted force state)"

expectations.text: Identifies that no startup path restores or exits a stale hold
expectations.evidence: "restore_force_mode_from_persistence (~L23640) gets None and early-returns"; "_restore_disabled_optimizer_reserve_if_stale (L117) hard-excludes sigenergy at L129 and is reserve-keyed — STANDBY never touches the reserve, so it's structurally invisible"

expectations.text: Notes the disabled-optimizer condition
expectations.evidence: "with the optimizer disabled there is no 5-minute cycle to overwrite it"; "Optimizer-enabled installs self-repair in ~5 min"

expectations.text: Fix outline includes persisting hold state with expiry and restoring/exiting on setup
expectations.evidence: "Persist hold_soc_state in persist_force_mode_state (mode 'hold_soc', absolute UTC expiry...)"; "Restore-or-exit in restore_force_mode_from_persistence: future expiry → re-arm timer... expired → brand restore with the _restore_pre_idle_backup_reserve retry contract" plus a runtime guard for existing installs

expectations.text: Identifies the Tesla EV-preserve fallback (_set_idle_hold_mode(preserve_charge=True)) raising reserve to ~SOC with _pre_idle_backup_reserve snapshot
expectations.evidence: "TeslaEnergyCoordinator has no set_no_discharge_mode ... → fallback _set_idle_hold_mode(battery, preserve_charge=True)"; "reserve = min(max(soc_pct, 0), 80) → set_backup_reserve(74)"; "snapshots _pre_idle_backup_reserve = _startup_backup_reserve (config 20%)"

expectations.text: Identifies disable() gating idle cleanup on _last_executed_action == 'idle' while marker is 'no_discharge'
expectations.evidence: "the reserve cleanup is gated on self._last_executed_action == 'idle' (:2940); marker is 'no_discharge' → _restore_pre_idle_backup_reserve never runs"

expectations.text: Identifies the EV release path restores work mode only, never the Tesla reserve
expectations.evidence: "falls through to battery_controller.restore_normal() → power_sync.restore_normal service, which restores a reserve only from force-state saved_backup_reserve or hold-SoC state — neither exists here → mode/tariff only"

expectations.text: Notes no later rescue (self-heal excludes Tesla, optimizer disabled)
expectations.evidence: "the per-cycle Tesla reserve reset needs the optimizer enabled; _restore_disabled_optimizer_reserve_if_stale excludes Tesla at line 129; and on re-enable the drift check can even adopt 74% as the user's reserve"

expectations.text: Fix outline restores pre-idle reserve in disable() whenever set (ungated from 'idle')
expectations.evidence: "disable(): run _restore_pre_idle_backup_reserve whenever _pre_idle_backup_reserve is not None (ungate from == 'idle')" plus release-side symmetry, adoption-path guard, and a runtime guard for already-stuck installs

expectations.text: Identifies _spread_export_schedule as the post-solve override that rewrites the slot
expectations.evidence: "_spread_export_schedule in .../optimization/coordinator.py (L5945) flattens planned export energy across each contiguous run of export-allowed slots"

expectations.text: Identifies spread_positions includes every slot filtered only by SOC, never by original action
expectations.evidence: "L6030 spread_positions = list(range(start, end)) takes every slot in the run, filtered only by soc > floor + 0.0001 ... L6100-6124 then emits ScheduleAction(action='export', ...) without checking original.action"

expectations.text: Identifies the positive-export-price allowed mask can span LP charge slots
expectations.evidence: "_positive_price_export_slots (L6264): every slot with export price > 0 is allowed. Flow Power's flat positive feed-in makes essentially the whole 48 h horizon one contiguous run spanning the overnight charge slots"

expectations.text: States gating conditions: spread_export_enabled + target-export-power brand

expectations.text: Fix outline excludes non-export/discharge original actions (preserves charge slots)

expectations.text: Identifies that hold_soc_state and its expiry timer are in-memory only and lost on restart/reload
expectations.evidence: "armed entirely in an in-memory closure dict with an in-memory expiry timer"; "a HA restart destroys both the hold record and its auto-restore timer"

expectations.text: Identifies that persist_force_mode_state serializes only force charge/discharge (Hold writes None)
expectations.evidence: "that function only serializes force_charge_state/force_discharge_state. With a hold active, state_to_save = None — it not only skips the hold, it actively writes force_mode_state = None"

expectations.text: Identifies that no startup path restores or exits a stale hold
expectations.evidence: "restore_force_mode_from_persistence() finds nothing and returns; it only understands mode in ('charge','discharge')"; "_restore_disabled_optimizer_reserve_if_stale explicitly excludes Sigenergy (line 129) and only fixes reserve, never EMS/work mode"

expectations.text: Notes the disabled-optimizer condition
expectations.evidence: "With Smart Optimization OFF, no LP cycle ever overwrites STANDBY → frozen forever"

expectations.text: Fix outline includes persisting hold state with expiry and restoring/exiting on setup
expectations.evidence: "Add a hold branch to persist_force_mode_state()..."; "extend restore_force_mode_from_persistence() with mode=='hold': re-arm state + timer... call restore_normal if expired" plus a stale-STANDBY runtime guard

expectations.text: Identifies the Tesla EV-preserve fallback (_set_idle_hold_mode(preserve_charge=True)) raising reserve to ~SOC with _pre_idle_backup_reserve snapshot
expectations.evidence: "TeslaEnergyCoordinator has no set_no_discharge_mode ... falls back to _set_idle_hold_mode(battery, preserve_charge=True)"; "saves the real reserve (20%) into in-memory _pre_idle_backup_reserve, then the Tesla branch sets reserve = min(max(soc_pct, 0), 80) = 74"

expectations.text: Identifies disable() gating idle cleanup on _last_executed_action == 'idle' while marker is 'no_discharge'
expectations.evidence: "line 2940's gate _last_executed_action == 'idle' can never be true during preserve, so _restore_pre_idle_backup_reserve is skipped"

expectations.text: Identifies the EV release path restores work mode only, never the Tesla reserve
expectations.evidence: "falls to battery_controller.restore_normal() → power_sync.restore_normal service, which no-ops with no active force state ... The release path is asymmetric: it never restores _pre_idle_backup_reserve"

expectations.text: Notes no later rescue (self-heal excludes Tesla, optimizer disabled)
expectations.evidence: "the polling safety net ... is cancelled by disable()"; "_restore_disabled_optimizer_reserve_if_stale explicitly excludes Tesla at line 129-130 ... Nothing ever writes 20% back"

expectations.text: Fix outline restores pre-idle reserve in disable() whenever set (ungated from 'idle')
expectations.evidence: "In disable(): after the no-discharge release, restore whenever _pre_idle_backup_reserve is not None (not only when last action was 'idle')"

expectations.text: Identifies _spread_export_schedule as the post-solve override that rewrites the slot
expectations.evidence: "OptimizationCoordinator._spread_export_schedule() ... (lines 5945-6161) is a post-solve rewrite ... rewrites every slot in the window, with no exclusion for charge actions"

expectations.text: Identifies spread_positions includes every slot filtered only by SOC, never by original action
expectations.evidence: "Line 6030: spread_positions = list(range(start, end)) — all slots in the allowed window, regardless of original action ... The SOC-floor filter cannot save a charge slot"

expectations.text: Identifies the positive-export-price allowed mask can span LP charge slots

expectations.evidence: "treat charge slots as window breakers (mirror line 5857 of the import spreader): skip/split on action == 'charge' ... exclude them from spread_positions"

analyst_observations: Non-discriminating on correctness: both configurations passed all 15 assertions (the underlying model diagnoses these three bugs unaided). The skill's measured value here is efficiency and process, not raw accuracy.

Efficiency: with-skill averaged 313s / 90.5k tokens / 23 tool uses vs baseline 443s / 96.6k / 37 — a 29% wall-clock reduction. The gap is widest where the registry short-circuits discovery (eval-0 hold-soc: 267s/20 tools vs 510s/41) and near-zero where the symptom names the feature outright (eval-2 spread-export).

Process differences visible only in with-skill outputs: registry cross-check then independent HEAD verification (never trusting the registry blindly), verification-playbook compensating-mechanism sweeps, user remediation guidance, and a registry-maintenance step in fix outlines.
Baseline runs executed in the same repo where the skill lives; no contamination observed (no baseline output references the registry or skill files).

Iteration-2 direction: add discriminating evals where correctness should diverge — symptoms matching REFUTED claims (e.g. phantom overnight solar / RC-3, or 'price hiccup wiped my plan' / RC-4) test whether the skill prevents confirming a plausible-but-wrong bug, which is its core design goal.
